summary: First iteration full-stack testing of NCC World Cup Prediction Contest. Backend pytest: 16/16 passed covering matches seed, auth idempotent register/login, admin auth (X-Admin-Password), predictions CRUD with auto-derived winner, status transitions blocking predictions, result submission + point computation (5 exact / 3 winner / 0 wrong), leaderboard anti-cheating (only finished matches counted), and locked /predictions/match/{id} until finished. Frontend Playwright E2E: home loads in Arabic RTL by default, language toggle flips dir to LTR and back, registration creates employee and redirects to /matches with user-name in header, prediction dialog accepts scores and shows winner, admin login with ncc-admin-2026 submits results, leaderboard populates after result, my-predictions reflects exact-score points (5) as shown in screenshot.
critical_code_review_comments: POST /api/admin/matches/{id}/status receives status as a query param (params=...) rather than JSON body — works but is slightly non-RESTful. Acceptable for MVP.
Register endpoint returns existing record on idempotent call (good) but does NOT update the name even when a different name is supplied — by design for anti-cheating but worth documenting.
compute_points relies on prediction's stored winner field, but submit_prediction overrides client winner with score-derived winner — consistent and correct.
Frontend Home.jsx uses window.location.href = '/register' instead of react-router navigate() — causes full page reload. Minor UX nit.
No rate limiting on /api/auth/register — anyone can create unlimited employees. Acceptable for internal company contest.
Admin password lives in localStorage on frontend (sent via X-Admin-Password). Fine for an internal contest but anyone with browser access could exfiltrate it.
test_credentials: Admin password: ncc-admin-2026. Test employees created on-the-fly (e.g. EID=E2E-<ts>, name=E2E Tester). Backend tests use TEST_E*_<uuid> prefixed IDs.
seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 20 World Cup 2026 matches on first startup; verified via GET /api/matches. E2E test created test employees and submitted one prediction + one admin result on a real seeded match.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All endpoints verified working. Anti-cheating rule confirmed: GET /api/leaderboard returns only points from finished matches, and GET /api/predictions/match/{id} is locked until match status='finished'. The E2E run leaves residual state: one match has status='finished' with result, and TEST_/E2E_ employees exist in MongoDB. If clean state needed, call POST /api/admin/reset (admin auth) then restart backend to re-seed. data-testid coverage is excellent — all interactive elements have stable testids.
rca of the issue: N/A — no blocking issues found. The transient `stat-points=0` readback in the Playwright log is a test-side race (text read before useEffect/API resolved) and not a product bug; the final screenshot shows the correct value of 5 points.
